sglang.title: Use an env var SGLANG_SET_CPU_AFFINITY to set cpu affinity; turn it off by default
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
It will conflict with some other libraries (e.g. Ray). We see an error message like below, so we want to turn it off by default.
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.10/multiprocessing/process.py", line 314, in _bootstrap
    self.run()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.10/multiprocess

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Hardware][CPU] Fix intel-omp version to avoid segfault
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The latest intel-omp will cause a segment fault on process exiting.
This PR fixed intel-omp version to avoid the problem.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Mamba] Fix Multistep on Mamba-like models
vllm.author: mzusman
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Bugfix for Mamba with multistep scheduler, where finished requests ids were not passed into the MambaCacheManager,
which in turn ran into out of free slots situation, unable to receive new incoming requests,
The fix was to move the get_and_reset_finished_request_call inside the if clause so it would

vllm.title: [Frontend] don't block event loop in tokenization (preprocess) in OpenAI compatible server
vllm.author: tomeras91
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, tokenization of requests in the OpenAI compatible server is done synchronously on the process running the server. Usually, this is not a problem, but when dealing with long sequence lengths (256K), tokenization can take a few hundred milliseconds. Combining long requests with high loads c

vllm.title: [Bug][CLI] Allow users to disable prefix caching explicitly
vllm.author: rickyyx
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
With the change, the behaviour would be:
V0:

if users specify --enable-prefix-caching => enabled
if users don't specify => disabled by default.

V1:

if users specify --no-enable-prefix-caching => enabled
if users don't specify => enabled by default.
